conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Good morning, Mr. Ahmed. I'm Doctor Sara. What brings you in today?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Good morning, doctor. I've been feeling unwell for the past two days. I have a runny nose and a sore throat.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: I see. Do you also have a cough or any fever?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Yes, I have a mild cough, and I felt a slight fever last night.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Is the cough dry, or are you bringing up mucus?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: It's mostly dry, but sometimes there's a little mucus.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Have you noticed any body aches or fatigue?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Yes, I feel tired all the time, and my body feels a bit achy.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Any headaches or sinus pressure?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Yes, I have a mild headache and some pressure around my nose.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Alright. Have you been in contact with anyone who was sick recently?

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Yes, a colleague at work had similar symptoms last week.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: That makes sense. Based on your symptoms — runny nose, sore throat, mild cough, slight fever, and fatigue — this looks like a common cold.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Is it serious, doctor?

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: No, it's usually mild and self-limiting. It should improve within a few days to a week.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Do I need antibiotics?

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: No, antibiotics are not effective for viral infections like the common cold. Instead, focus on rest and hydration.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: What can I take to feel better?

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: You can take paracetamol for fever and pain, use saline nasal spray for congestion, and drink warm fluids. Honey can help soothe your throat.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Alright. Should I stay home from work?

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: Yes, it's better to rest and avoid spreading the infection to others, especially for the next couple of days.

conversation.speaker: patient
conversation.text: Okay, doctor. Thank you.

conversation.speaker: doctor
conversation.text: You're welcome. If your symptoms worsen, or if you develop a high fever or difficulty breathing, come back immediately.
